However, this is minimal and not indicative of actual risk.\n   - The confidence levels are high (1), which is appropriate.\n\n5. **Overall Conclusion:**\n   - The code is standard, generated boilerplate for module re-exporting.\n   - No signs of malicious activity, obfuscation, or security vulnerabilities.\n   - The scores assigned in the reports are reasonable and consistent with the code.\n\n**Final Recommendations:**\n- Based on the code and reports, I would assign the following scores:\n  - **Malware:** 0\n  - **Obfuscated:** 0\n  - **Risk:** 0 (or very close to zero, e.g., 0.1 if considering minimal boilerplate, but generally 0)\n\n**Summary:**\nThis supply chain security incident report appears to be a false alarm or a misunderstanding of boilerplate transpiled code.
